title: Most podman commands as user abort with "Error: cannot re-exec process"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Most podman commands as user abort with "Error: cannot re-exec process". E.g. `podman ps`. Using a **rootless** setup.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
Not fully sure how to reproduce this reliably. It seems to happen under high load. E.g. after rebooting a server with hundreds of rootless pods, about 10% will show this error. The root cause is a leftover PID file in `/run/user/$UID/libpod/tmp/pause.pid`, as described [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8647#issuecomment-881911751). The process in this PID file doesn't exist. Maybe it crashed or was killed for some reason.

**Describe the results you received:**
All Podman commands fail with "Error: cannot re-exec process". If Systemd is set up for this container, it will constantly try to restart, which causes high CPU load on the server. Simply deleting `pause.pid` resolves the error.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman should recover from a stale PID file.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.5

Built:      Fri Mar 18 11:51:13 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes. No related item found.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Centos Stream 9 with all updates applied and SELinux enabled.

`/run/user` folders are mounted as tmpfs and cleared during reboot.

```
tmpfs on /run/user/4275 type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,seclabel,size=26328568k,nr_inodes=6582142,mode=700,uid=4275,gid=4275,inode64)
tmpfs on /run/user/4276 type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,seclabel,size=26328568k,nr_inodes=6582142,mode=700,uid=4276,gid=4276,inode64)
```

comments.body: could you verify if catatonit supports the `-P` flag?  Does `/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit -P` work fine for you (you can Ctrl-C the process once you verify it works)?
Does it work in permissive mode? Does it work as root.
I guess it works as root as it doesn't need a pause process
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe What should we do with this issue?
we got no feedback, let's close for now

Short update on this: Restarted another server today (CentOS 9) with the same setup and there was no issue with this. It has about 700 rootless pods and they all came up flawlessly. There wasn't a single stale pause.pid file to be found by my script.

Thanks anyways for looking into this! 🙏
